deep_dive.title: Joint analysis of multiply perturbed cells improves statistical power and cost efficiency in Perturb-seq
deep_dive.abstract: Perturb-seq measures transcriptomic responses to genetic perturbations at scale, but conventional designs that enrich for one guide RNA per cell remain resource-intensive. Standard analyses discard cells carrying multiple guides, further limiting the usable yield from each experiment. Here, we characterize how incorporating these guide multiplets affects signal recovery, information loss, and cost reduction. At the highest guide burden, cells showed increased stress and suppressed cell-cycle progression. We develop PerturbMatch, a scalable statistical framework to analyze guide multiplets. Among different classes of guide multiplets, doublets and triplets recovered perturbation responses more accurately than higher-order multiplets. Across three 5000-gene Perturb-seq screens with increasing guide loading, per-cell costs decreased by up to 81% while information loss remained within 1.5-fold of the loss observed between technical replicates. In existing genome-wide Perturb-seq data, incorporating previously discarded guide multiplets increased usable cell numbers and improved statistical power. Compared with a singlet holdout set, adding guide multiplets moved signal recovery closer to the theoretical expected reproducibility. Overall, we recommend a design that intentionally includes single-guide cells, guide doublets, and guide triplets to improve cost efficiency while preserving signal recovery.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: analyzes multiply perturbed cells in Perturb-seq for perturbation response
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 分析Perturb-seq中多次扰动细胞的扰动响应
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 分析Perturb-seq中多次扰动细胞的扰动响应
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 分析Perturb-seq中多次扰动细胞的扰动响应
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PerturbMatch enables joint analysis of multiply perturbed cells in Perturb-seq, improving statistical power and cost efficiency by recovering perturbation responses from doublets and triplets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文针对Perturb-seq中传统方法丢弃多向导RNA细胞的问题，提出PerturbMatch框架，能够有效利用这些细胞信息。结果表明，细胞双联体和三联体能更准确地恢复扰动响应，同时降低实验成本。该方法提高了扰动响应预测的统计功效。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文针对Perturb-seq中传统方法丢弃多向导RNA细胞的问题，提出PerturbMatch框架，能够有效利用这些细胞信息。结果表明，细胞双联体和三联体能更准确地恢复扰动响应，同时降低实验成本。该方法提高了扰动响应预测的统计功效。

quick_skim.title: Identifying cancer cell-state transitions from multimodal single-cell data
quick_skim.abstract: Phenotypic plasticity allows cancer cells to evade therapy, yet the transient nature of state transitions has made their molecular drivers difficult to define. Here, we present a single-cell framework that leverages the temporal delays between mRNA and protein accumulation to directly capture cells undergoing phenotypic switching. We show that differentiation-associated delays between transcript and protein accumulation are detectable in multimodal single-cell data as discordant mRNA and surface-protein levels. Applying this strategy to the K562 leukemia model, which alternates between differentiated CD24- and progenitor-like CD24+ states, we identify transitioning cells and derive a transcriptional signature linking cell-cycle progression and mitochondrial remodeling to plasticity. Genome-wide CRISPR screening confirms key regulators of plasticity, including BCR-ABL1 and mitochondrial homeostasis genes. We summarize the transition-associated program into a score that predicts imatinib response in chronic myeloid leukemia, stratifies survival in acute myeloid leukemia, and retains prognostic value across 31 TCGA tumor types. Spatial transcriptomics reveals localized plasticity hotspots in solid tumors. Together, this framework exposes the molecular basis of cancer plasticity and enables its quantification across tumors.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: identifies cancer cell-state transitions using multimodal single-cell data, relevant to perturbation response dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用多模态单细胞数据识别癌症细胞状态转变，与扰动响应动态相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用多模态单细胞数据识别癌症细胞状态转变，与扰动响应动态相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用多模态单细胞数据识别癌症细胞状态转变，与扰动响应动态相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A single-cell framework leveraging mRNA-protein temporal delays to directly capture cells undergoing phenotypic switching, linking cell-cycle and mitochondrial stress to state transitions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 癌症细胞状态转变的瞬时性使其驱动因素难以定义。本文利用单细胞多模态数据中mRNA与蛋白积累的时间延迟，直接捕获处于表型转换中的细胞。在K562白血病模型中识别出过渡细胞，并推导出连接细胞周期进展和线粒体应激的转录特征。该方法为分析扰动后细胞状态变化提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 癌症细胞状态转变的瞬时性使其驱动因素难以定义。本文利用单细胞多模态数据中mRNA与蛋白积累的时间延迟，直接捕获处于表型转换中的细胞。在K562白血病模型中识别出过渡细胞，并推导出连接细胞周期进展和线粒体应激的转录特征。该方法为分析扰动后细胞状态变化提供了新工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找与扰动响应预测/虚拟细胞相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: perturbation response prediction; virtual cell model; single cell perturbation; perturbation modeling; Predicting cellular response to genetic perturbations; Virtual cell models for drug or genetic perturbation; Deep learning methods for perturbation response prediction; Single cell perturbation response prediction.
